name: Maple Macadamia Biscotti
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a food processor; process to combine.
2. In a glass measuring cup, stir together remaining ingredients, except nuts and milk. With the machine running, add liquids to flour mixture; process until just combined. Add nuts; pulse to distribute evenly.
3. Transfer dough to an oblong storage container; cover and chill several hours or overnight.
4. Invert container on a flour-dusted board; remove dough. Cut dough in half down the middle.
5. With flour dusted hands, pat each half into a log about 3 to 12 inches long. Transfer logs to separate baking sheets that have been coated with nonstick cooking spray. Brush tops lightly with milk.
6. Bake in a preheated 350-degree oven for 20 to 24 minutes, until firm and light brown. Remove from oven and cool on pans for 2 minutes.
7. With two large spatulas, transfer logs to a cutting board; cool about 4 to 6 minutes longer.
8. Reduce oven temperature to 325 degrees. Using a gently sawing motion with a serrated knife, cut logs into 1/2-inch thick slices. Place cut side down on an ungreased baking sheet. Return to oven and bake for 10 to 12 minutes per side, until crisp and dry. Cool on wire racks.
